understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game is to complete each level by placing all selectable pieces onto goal tiles (marked '+'). Every goal must be occupied simultaneously to trigger a win, and the entire game is won after finishing all levels within a limited step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movements (MOVE_UP, MOVE_DOWN, MOVE_LEFT, MOVE_RIGHT) shift the currently selected piece in pixel coordinates (3px per cell). Some objects (blocks) can be pushed when adjacent. Walls ('#') may block movement in various ways, requiring trial and error. The 'CLICK' action changes the selected piece to another selectable at the given target_position (in the same pixel coordinates). The semantic_grid visualises the cell-level state: P for selected piece, S for other selectables, B for blocks, # for walls, + for goals, and . for empty cells. The 'movement.direction_blocked' hints are just warnings of adjacency, not absolute blockers. Steps are limited per level, so efficient moves are essential.
